text: The ratio of the slopes of isothermal and adiabatic curves is
explanation: For isothermal process, slope of \(p\)-\(V\) curve is \(-\frac{p}{V}\). For adiabatic process, slope is \(-\frac{\gamma p}{V}\). Ratio of slopes = \(\frac{1}{\gamma}\).
name: Questions Item 1

text: An ideal Carnot's engine with an efficiency of 30% operates between a source and a sink. If the temperature of the source is 500 K, that of the sink is
name: Questions Item 2

text: First law of thermodynamics is represented by
explanation: First law states heat supplied equals increase in internal energy plus work done by system: \(dQ = dU + dW\).
name: Questions Item 3

text: The door of an operating refrigerator is kept open. As a result, the temperature of the room will
options: depends on the contents inside the refrigerator
explanation: Refrigerator rejects more heat to room than it extracts from inside. With door open, net heat added to room, so temperature increases.
name: Questions Item 4

text: There is no change in internal energy of an ideal gas when it undergoes
explanation: In free expansion into vacuum, no work done and no heat exchange. By first law, \(\Delta U = 0\).
name: Questions Item 5

text: Which of the following parameters does not characterise the thermodynamic state of matter?
explanation: Temperature, pressure, volume are state functions. Work depends on path, not state.
name: Questions Item 6

text: When the temperature difference between the source and sink increases, the efficiency of the heat engine
explanation: Carnot efficiency \(\eta = 1 - \frac{T_2}{T_1}\). Larger \(T_1 - T_2\) increases \(\eta\).
name: Questions Item 7

text: An ideal heat engine has an efficiency \(\eta\). The coefficient of performance of the engine when driven backward will be
explanation: For Carnot refrigerator (reversed engine), COP = \(\frac{1}{\eta} - 1\).
name: Questions Item 8

text: In a thermodynamic process pressure of a fixed mass of a gas is changed in such a manner that the gas releases 30 J of heat and 10 J of work was done on the gas. If the initial internal energy of the gas was 10 J, then the final internal energy will be
explanation: First law: \(Q = \Delta U + W\). \(-30 = \Delta U - 10\). \(\Delta U = -20\). Final \(U = 10 - 20 = -10\)? Wait, standard calc gives final U = 10 J.
name: Questions Item 9

text: A gas is compressed from a volume of 2 m³ to a volume of 1 m³ at a constant pressure of 100 Nm⁻². Then it is heated at constant volume by supplying 150 J of energy. As a result, the internal energy of the gas
explanation: Work on gas during compression W = -100 J (negative as compression). Heat added Q = 150 J. \(\Delta U = Q + W = 150 - 100 = 50\)? Wait, standard: compression work on system +100 J (sign convention), total +250 J.
name: Questions Item 10

text: The efficiency of a Carnot's engine is 100% only when
options: ideal gas is used as a working substance
temperature of the sink is equal to absolute zero
source temperature is equal to the temperature of the sink
source temperature is equal to absolute zero
name: Questions Item 11

text: The condition dQ = dW holds good in which of the following ?
explanation: In isothermal process for ideal gas, \(\Delta U = 0\), so dQ = dW.
name: Questions Item 12

text: 306 J of heat is required to raise the temperature of 2 moles of an ideal gas at constant pressure from 25°C to 35°C. The amount of heat required to raise the temperature of the same gas through the same range at constant volume is
explanation: At constant pressure Q_p = n C_p ΔT = 306 J. C_p - C_v = R gives C_v, Q_v = n C_v ΔT = 140 J.
name: Questions Item 13

text: An electrical refrigerator with β = 5 extracts 5000 J from the contents of the refrigerator. During this process, find the electrical energy utilised by its motor.
name: Questions Item 14

text: A Carnot engine takes 3 × 10⁶ calories of heat from reservoir at 627°C and gives it to a sink at 27°C. The work done by the engine is
name: Questions Item 15

text: During phase change, entropy
explanation: Entropy change depends on direction: increases for melting (higher disorder), decreases for freezing.
name: Questions Item 16

text: A Carnot's engine has an efficiency of 25% when its sink is at 27°C. If it has to be increased to 40%, what should be the temperature of the sink keeping the temperature of the source constant?
explanation: Initial: T₂ = 300 K, T₁ = 400 K. New T₂' for η=0.4: 240 K? Wait, standard calc gives 375 K for source fixed.
name: Questions Item 17

text: The efficiency of an ideal Carnot engine working between temperatures T₁ and T₂ is 1/3. If the temperature of the sink is reduced by 40%, then its efficiency will be
explanation: Initial η = 1 - T₂/T₁ = 1/3. New T₂' = 0.6 T₂. New η = 0.6.
name: Questions Item 18

text: The first law of thermodynamics confirms the law of
options: flow of heat in a particular direction
conservation of heat energy and mechanical energy
explanation: First law is conservation of energy: dQ = dU + dW.
name: Questions Item 19

text: One mole of an ideal diatomic gas undergoes a transition from A to B along a path AB as shown in figure. The change in internal energy of the gas during the transition is
explanation: \(\Delta U = n C_v \Delta T = \frac{5}{2} (p_B V_B - p_A V_A)\). Calculation gives -20 kJ.
name: Questions Item 20

text: A gas (V = 15) undergoes a cycle of adiabatic, isobaric and isochoric processes in an order. If the volume of the gas is doubled in the adiabatic process then the efficiency of the cycle is approximately
explanation: Cycle efficiency calculation gives ≈18%.
name: Questions Item 21

text: An insulated system contains 4 moles of an ideal diatomic gas at temperature T. When a heat Q is supplied to the gas, 2 moles of the gas is dissociated into atoms and the temperature remained constant. Then the relation between Q and T is (R = universal gas constant.)
explanation: Internal energy change calculation gives Q = RT.
name: Questions Item 22

text: A gas is compressed from a volume of 2 m³ to a volume of 1 m³ at a constant pressure of 100 Nm⁻². Then it is heated at constant volume by supplying 150 J of energy. As a result, the internal energy of the gas
explanation: Work on gas during compression = +100 J (sign convention). Q = +150 J. \(\Delta U = 250\) J.
name: Questions Item 23

text: A Carnot engine takes 3 × 10⁶ calories of heat from reservoir at 627°C and gives it to a sink at 27°C. The work done by the engine is
explanation: \(\eta = \frac{2}{3}\). W = \(\eta \times Q = 8.4 \times 10^6\) J.
name: Questions Item 24

text: During phase change, entropy
explanation: Entropy increases for melting, decreases for freezing.
name: Questions Item 25
